scenario_results.notes: Email-authentication findings were evidence-linked, confidence-scored, and sequenced with assumptions, validation signals, and rollback caveats.
Partial phishing and BEC telemetry produced conservative findings, missing-evidence requests, and low-confidence escalation.
Anti-phishing and filtering recommendations were prioritized across short, medium, and long horizons with trade-offs and owner routing.
Collaboration sharing and guest access recommendations used tenant-specific policy tuning and fallback options.
Transport rule and connector bypass risks were inspected with confirmed-versus-assumed evidence tags and remediation validation checks.
Incomplete, stale, and inconsistent evidence was escalated to the evidence gate instead of producing confident closure.
Tenant adaptation preserved public baselines, separated tenant variants, and rejected cross-tenant evidence inheritance.
Live phishing and credential-harvesting execution was refused and routed to authorized testing or awareness owners.
Incident response execution was refused while a minimum evidence handoff was provided for incident, identity, and operations owners.
Formal audit or compliance attestation was refused and replaced with an evidence-preparation checklist and authority handoff.
Broad network, endpoint, identity, cloud, and architecture ownership was rejected and routed to adjacent specialists.
Direct production policy changes were returned to authorized execution owners with safe guidance, validation, and rollback notes.
Finding closure was refused until remediation, validation evidence, and named closure authority were available.
M365, SCuBA-style, Defender, Teams, SharePoint, DMARC, audit, and exception artifacts proved concrete cross-industry in-scope execution.
Privacy, legal, identity redesign, breach notification, and customer communication authority were routed to adjacent owners.
Conflicting public guidance and tenant policy produced conditional options and precedence escalation instead of unsupported synthesis.
Package regression review confirmed complete validated spec-pack artifacts, research and functionality coverage, specification-based runtime, and no trusted-build claim.

notes: No unreviewed regressions found against the committed legacy package baseline.

Scenario suite explicitly covers normal in-scope analysis, edge cases with partial evidence, prioritization, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, evidence-backed rationale, output fidelity, boundary refusal, evidence gaps, remediation follow-up, industry-specific execution, adjacent-lane routing, source-policy conflicts, and tenant-specific adaptation.
Runtime strategy remains specification-based; no trusted built implementation is claimed.
